# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import * as jsonwebtoken from 'jsonwebtoken';  //npm install @types/jsonwebtoken --save-dev

const express = require('express');   // npm i express
var bodyParser=require("body-parser");//to support JSON-encoded bodies for PUT and POST requests.       	
                           
export const authMiddleware: (req, res) => {(token): any=> {} } =  async function middlewareImplementation(){   //typescript syntax to declare a variable that can hold your value asynchronously during execution 
    return new Promise((resolve , reject )=> 
        jsonwebtoken.decode( token,'SECRET_KEY', (err, decoded) => {     	        		               				                    if(!decoded){//if not a user or has invalid credentials then do something }else{            //parse the payload from JWT             resolve({userId: decodeJwt.id});        }} 
    );   };     return middlewareImplementation;                                                                                            });};                                                    		          'use strict';                                     var express = require('express');     	                                   app=require("./app");                                  const port = 3001 ;      									                 // Creating a server                          let server: Http.Server<typeof Request, typeof Response>
     if(!server) {                  	       		   console . log ('Could not listen on port : ' + port);                return;                                                        }          Console           (` Server is running at http://localhost： ${port} / ` );        }); 	}); };									   	                	} else            
});			    	  }} // The JWTs will be added in the request header with name 'authorization' where {token: token } and used as a middleware. This approach avoid dealing secret management for production but still provide access to protected routes (unauthenticated requests)  });                    var app = express();    	        
app . use(bodyParser.json());      //to support JSON-encoded bodies                     	});  		                                                                                     if(!process    'env'hasJwtSecretKey){//if the JWT_SECRET is not set then do something  throw new Error('Please define your jw t key in environment variable'); }
        require("./jwtAuth");                  	app.listen(port, () => { console . log (` Server started at http://localhost:${ port} `); });     					});    };})()); //This is how you call the middleware from a route handler to use JWTs in requests
